trans-unit.source: Exercise 1: Health site distribution in the Saint Louis region
trans-unit.target: Exercise 1: Health site distribution in the Saint Louis region

trans-unit.source: Characteristics of the exercise
trans-unit.target: Characteristics of the exercise

trans-unit.source: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Aim of the exercise:__ ^^^
trans-unit.target: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Aim of the exercise:__ ^^^

trans-unit.source: Become familiar with different types of spatial analysis and geoprocessing tools. Understand the process of discovering relationships and connections between features in spatial data.
trans-unit.target: Become familiar with different types of spatial analysis and geoprocessing tools. Understand the process of discovering relationships and connections between features in spatial data.

trans-unit.source: __Type of trainings exercise:__
trans-unit.target: __Type of trainings exercise:__

trans-unit.source: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.
trans-unit.target: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.

trans-unit.source: It can be done as a follow-along exercise or individually as a self-study.
trans-unit.target: It can be done as a follow-along exercise or individually as a self-study.

trans-unit.source: __These skills are relevant for:__
trans-unit.target: __These skills are relevant for:__

trans-unit.source: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Estimated time demand for the exercise:__ ^^^
trans-unit.target: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Estimated time demand for the exercise:__ ^^^

trans-unit.source: [Geodata Import in QGIS](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Geodata Import in QGIS](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: [Projections](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Projections](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: [Spatial Queries](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_queries_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Spatial Queries](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_queries_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: [Geoprocessing](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geoprocessing_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Geoprocessing](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geoprocessing_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: [Categorized classification](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_categorized_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Categorized classification](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_categorized_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: Instructions for the trainers
trans-unit.target: Instructions for the trainers

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__

trans-unit.source: Prepare the training
trans-unit.target: Prepare the training

trans-unit.source: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.
trans-unit.target: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.

trans-unit.source: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.
trans-unit.target: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.

trans-unit.source: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.
trans-unit.target: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.

trans-unit.source: Check out [How to do trainings?](/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.md) for some general tips on training conduction
trans-unit.target: Check out [How to do trainings?](/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.md) for some general tips on training conduction

trans-unit.source: Conduct the training
trans-unit.target: Conduct the training

trans-unit.source: __Introduction:__
trans-unit.target: __Introduction:__

trans-unit.source: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.
trans-unit.target: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.

trans-unit.source: __Follow-along:__
trans-unit.target: __Follow-along:__

trans-unit.source: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.
trans-unit.target: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.

trans-unit.source: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: __Wrap up:__
trans-unit.target: __Wrap up:__

trans-unit.source: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Leave some time for open questions.
trans-unit.target: Leave some time for open questions.

trans-unit.source: Data
trans-unit.target: Data

trans-unit.source: Download the datasets [here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Module_5/Exercise_1/Module_5_Exercise_1_Healthsite_distribution_Saint_Louis_region.zip) and unzip the files.
trans-unit.target: Download the datasets [here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Module_5/Exercise_1/Module_5_Exercise_1_Healthsite_distribution_Saint_Louis_region.zip) and unzip the files.

trans-unit.source: Task
trans-unit.target: Task

trans-unit.source: The goal of this exercise will be to assess the health site distribution in the Saint Louis region in Senegal and determine which health sites are prone to flooding.
trans-unit.target: The goal of this exercise will be to assess the health site distribution in the Saint Louis region in Senegal and determine which health sites are prone to flooding.

trans-unit.source: Load the health sites layer (`sen_healthsites.shp`) and the administrative boundary data (`sen_admbnda_adm1_1m_gov_ocha_20190426.shp`) into your QGIS project. Add OpenStreetMap as a background layer (Hint: XYZ via the browser window or the QuickMapServices plugin).
trans-unit.target: Load the health sites layer (`sen_healthsites.shp`) and the administrative boundary data (`sen_admbnda_adm1_1m_gov_ocha_20190426.shp`) into your QGIS project. Add OpenStreetMap as a background layer (Hint: XYZ via the browser window or the QuickMapServices plugin).

trans-unit.source: Select all health sites which are located within the Saint Louis region:
trans-unit.target: Select all health sites which are located within the Saint Louis region:

trans-unit.source: Select the region `Saint Louis` in the boundary layer. You can just manually select the specific rows. See the Wiki entry on [spatial queries](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_queries_wiki.md) for further information.
trans-unit.target: Select the region `Saint Louis` in the boundary layer. You can just manually select the specific rows. See the Wiki entry on [spatial queries](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_queries_wiki.md) for further information.

trans-unit.source: Save the selection under the new name `Saint_Louis_region` in an extra layer. This can be done by right clicking on the boundary layer → `Export` → `Save Selected Features As...`
trans-unit.target: Save the selection under the new name `Saint_Louis_region` in an extra layer. This can be done by right clicking on the boundary layer → `Export` → `Save Selected Features As...`

trans-unit.source: Exporting the selection to a new file. :::
trans-unit.target: Exporting the selection to a new file. :::

trans-unit.source: Use the ![](/fig/mAlgorithmSelectLocation.png) `Select by location` or ![](/fig/mAlgorithmClip.png) `Clip` tool to select all health facilities within the `Saint_Louis_region` layer.
trans-unit.target: Use the ![](/fig/mAlgorithmSelectLocation.png) `Select by location` or ![](/fig/mAlgorithmClip.png) `Clip` tool to select all health facilities within the `Saint_Louis_region` layer.

trans-unit.source: Save the new selection under the new name `healthsites_Saint_Louis` in an extra layer (Hint: right click on layer, Export, ...).
trans-unit.target: Save the new selection under the new name `healthsites_Saint_Louis` in an extra layer (Hint: right click on layer, Export, ...).

trans-unit.source: Screenshot of the select by location tool. :::
trans-unit.target: Screenshot of the select by location tool. :::

trans-unit.source: Investigate the flood risk in Saint Louis. Following the successful selection of all health sites in the Saint Louis region in the previous step, proceed to load the flood extent layer of Saint Louis into QGIS:
trans-unit.target: Investigate the flood risk in Saint Louis. Following the successful selection of all health sites in the Saint Louis region in the previous step, proceed to load the flood extent layer of Saint Louis into QGIS:

trans-unit.source: Add the flood extent layer (`EO4SD_SAINT_LOUIS_FLOOD_2018.shp`)
trans-unit.target: Add the flood extent layer (`EO4SD_SAINT_LOUIS_FLOOD_2018.shp`)

trans-unit.source: While the layer does not cover the entirety of the Saint Louis region, it extends beyond Saint Louis in the north. Use the ![](/fig/mAlgorithmClip.png) `Clip` tool to clip the flood extent layer to the Saint Louis region (Hint: as input use Saint Louis flood extent, use Saint_louis_region as the overlay) for a more concentrated focus on central Saint Louis. See the Wiki entry on [Geoprocessing](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geoprocessing_wiki.md) for further information.
trans-unit.target: While the layer does not cover the entirety of the Saint Louis region, it extends beyond Saint Louis in the north. Use the ![](/fig/mAlgorithmClip.png) `Clip` tool to clip the flood extent layer to the Saint Louis region (Hint: as input use Saint Louis flood extent, use Saint_louis_region as the overlay) for a more concentrated focus on central Saint Louis. See the Wiki entry on [Geoprocessing](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geoprocessing_wiki.md) for further information.

trans-unit.source: Save the selection as `Saint_Louis_flood_clipped`.
trans-unit.target: Save the selection as `Saint_Louis_flood_clipped`.

trans-unit.source: When looking at the attribute table of the `Saint_Louis_flood_clipped` layer (Watertype column), you will see that the layer includes flooded, non-flooded and water body areas.
trans-unit.target: When looking at the attribute table of the `Saint_Louis_flood_clipped` layer (Watertype column), you will see that the layer includes flooded, non-flooded and water body areas.

trans-unit.source: Screenshot of the Saint_Louis_flood_clipped layer. :::
trans-unit.target: Screenshot of the Saint_Louis_flood_clipped layer. :::

trans-unit.source: Visualize only the flooded areas and water bodies in the dataset:
trans-unit.target: Visualize only the flooded areas and water bodies in the dataset:

trans-unit.source: Go into `Symbology` and change the first selection from "Single symbols" to `Categorized`.
trans-unit.target: Go into `Symbology` and change the first selection from "Single symbols" to `Categorized`.

trans-unit.source: Select the column `Watertype` and click `Classify`.
trans-unit.target: Select the column `Watertype` and click `Classify`.

trans-unit.source: Choose different colors for both the water bodies and the flooded areas. Leave non-flooded areas unmarked or make them transparent (opacity = 0%).
trans-unit.target: Choose different colors for both the water bodies and the flooded areas. Leave non-flooded areas unmarked or make them transparent (opacity = 0%).

trans-unit.source: Visual exploration: Which areas are more and less prone to flooding?
trans-unit.target: Visual exploration: Which areas are more and less prone to flooding?

trans-unit.source: Screenshot of the water bodies and flooded areas. :::
trans-unit.target: Screenshot of the water bodies and flooded areas. :::

trans-unit.source: Assess which health sites are prone to flood risk.
trans-unit.target: Assess which health sites are prone to flood risk.

trans-unit.source: Make use of the [`Select by expression` tool]() to select all flooded areas in the `Saint_Louis_flood_clipped` layer. You can access this tool through the attribute table by clicking on this symbol ![](/fig/mIconExpressionSelect_new.png).
trans-unit.target: Make use of the [`Select by expression` tool]() to select all flooded areas in the `Saint_Louis_flood_clipped` layer. You can access this tool through the attribute table by clicking on this symbol ![](/fig/mIconExpressionSelect_new.png).

trans-unit.source: In the expression window on the left, enter the following expression:
trans-unit.target: In the expression window on the left, enter the following expression:

trans-unit.source: Selecting by expression: In this case we want the field (column) "WATERTYPE" to equal to 'Flooded'. :::
trans-unit.target: Selecting by expression: In this case we want the field (column) "WATERTYPE" to equal to 'Flooded'. :::

trans-unit.source: :::{admonition} Working with the Expression builder Writing your own expressions can be difficult at first. QGIS has a few quality of life features that can help you write a valid expression.
trans-unit.target: :::{admonition} Working with the Expression builder Writing your own expressions can be difficult at first. QGIS has a few quality of life features that can help you write a valid expression.

trans-unit.source: In the middle section of the expression builder window, you can expand various options. Look into "Fields and Values" to display the fields (columns) of that layer. Select the "WATERTYPE" field and <kbd>double-click</kbd> on it to add it to the expression box on the left.
trans-unit.target: In the middle section of the expression builder window, you can expand various options. Look into "Fields and Values" to display the fields (columns) of that layer. Select the "WATERTYPE" field and <kbd>double-click</kbd> on it to add it to the expression box on the left.

trans-unit.source: On the right side, you can preview the values of a field you've selected. Click on `All Unique` in order to see all the unique values of the "WATERTYPE"-column. By double clicking on a value, you can add it to the expression box on the left.
trans-unit.target: On the right side, you can preview the values of a field you've selected. Click on `All Unique` in order to see all the unique values of the "WATERTYPE"-column. By double clicking on a value, you can add it to the expression box on the left.

trans-unit.source: Notice how the fields are marked using double quotes marks (`"WATERTYPE"`), whereas values are marked with single quotes (`'FLOODED'`).
trans-unit.target: Notice how the fields are marked using double quotes marks (`"WATERTYPE"`), whereas values are marked with single quotes (`'FLOODED'`).

trans-unit.source: Under the expression box, there are buttons with common operators you can use in the expression builder.
trans-unit.target: Under the expression box, there are buttons with common operators you can use in the expression builder.

trans-unit.source: Below the expression box, you can see a Preview of the expression output. If the expression is invalid, you will see a red text "Expression is invalid" with an option to see what exactly is wrong in the syntax by clicking on "more info". :::
trans-unit.target: Below the expression box, you can see a Preview of the expression output. If the expression is invalid, you will see a red text "Expression is invalid" with an option to see what exactly is wrong in the syntax by clicking on "more info". :::

trans-unit.source: Save the selection in a separate layer. Name it `Saint_Louis_flooded_areas`. See [Export Selection](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_queries_wiki.html#export-selection) in the wiki for more information.
trans-unit.target: Save the selection in a separate layer. Name it `Saint_Louis_flooded_areas`. See [Export Selection](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_queries_wiki.html#export-selection) in the wiki for more information.

trans-unit.source: You can now remove the `Saint_Louis_flood_clipped` layer to avoid confusion.
trans-unit.target: You can now remove the `Saint_Louis_flood_clipped` layer to avoid confusion.

trans-unit.source: Make use of the ![](/fig/mAlgorithmSelectLocation.png) `Select by location` tool to assess which health sites are prone to flooding because they are located within the flooded areas in Saint Louis (Hint: Select features from: `healthsites_Saint_Louis`; By comparing to: `Saint_Louis_flooded_areas`).
trans-unit.target: Make use of the ![](/fig/mAlgorithmSelectLocation.png) `Select by location` tool to assess which health sites are prone to flooding because they are located within the flooded areas in Saint Louis (Hint: Select features from: `healthsites_Saint_Louis`; By comparing to: `Saint_Louis_flooded_areas`).

trans-unit.source: Screenshot of the Select by location tool. :::
trans-unit.target: Screenshot of the Select by location tool. :::

trans-unit.source: Check the attribute table of the `healthsites` layer for the selected features were one pharmacy and one hospital should be selected.
trans-unit.target: Check the attribute table of the `healthsites` layer for the selected features were one pharmacy and one hospital should be selected.

trans-unit.source: Screenshot of the selected health sites in the flooded areas. :::
trans-unit.target: Screenshot of the selected health sites in the flooded areas. :::

trans-unit.source: Which health sites are located close to flooded zones?
trans-unit.target: Which health sites are located close to flooded zones?

trans-unit.source: Create a ![](/fig/mAlgorithmBuffer.png) buffer around the health sites in Saint Louis with a distance of `20 meters`. See the Wiki entry on [Geoprocessing](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geoprocessing_wiki.md) for further information.
trans-unit.target: Create a ![](/fig/mAlgorithmBuffer.png) buffer around the health sites in Saint Louis with a distance of `20 meters`. See the Wiki entry on [Geoprocessing](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geoprocessing_wiki.md) for further information.

trans-unit.source: Screenshot of the buffer process :::
trans-unit.target: Screenshot of the buffer process :::

trans-unit.source: Now make use of the ![](/fig/mAlgorithmSelectLocation.png) `Select by location` tool again to assess which buffers intersect with the flooded areas.
trans-unit.target: Now make use of the ![](/fig/mAlgorithmSelectLocation.png) `Select by location` tool again to assess which buffers intersect with the flooded areas.

trans-unit.source: Screenshot of the Select by location tool for the buffered areas :::
trans-unit.target: Screenshot of the Select by location tool for the buffered areas :::

trans-unit.source: How many health sites are selected? Check the attribute table. The selection should include five pharmacies and one hospital.
trans-unit.target: How many health sites are selected? Check the attribute table. The selection should include five pharmacies and one hospital.

trans-unit.source: Feel free to experiment with additional buffer distances.
trans-unit.target: Feel free to experiment with additional buffer distances.

trans-unit.source: Screenshot of the buffered and selected health sites in the flooded areas :::
trans-unit.target: Screenshot of the buffered and selected health sites in the flooded areas :::

trans-unit.source: The final result may be represented as follows: it will display the flooded areas, the buffered health sites, and the intersections between them. While these features might not be easily visible in QGIS, you can verify that everything worked correctly by checking the attribute table.
trans-unit.target: The final result may be represented as follows: it will display the flooded areas, the buffered health sites, and the intersections between them. While these features might not be easily visible in QGIS, you can verify that everything worked correctly by checking the attribute table.

trans-unit.source: Screenshot of the final result :::
trans-unit.target: Screenshot of the final result :::
